metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_311
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig 2, Fig 5 NPR directly binds to the GluA1-N terminal extracellular domain
Fig 1 NPR stimulates assembly of both excitatory and inhibitory postsynaptic sites in a co-culture system via GluA1 binding
Fig 4, Fig 11 NPR regulates surface GluA1 levels, and its loss impairs excitatory synaptic transmission without affecting the numbers of synapses formed between neurons.. Experimental description: Mouse hippocampal neurons were analyzed.

Fig.1+2A: human cells + human NPR (flag tagged)
Fig.2B-D+4: human cells + mouse NPR-ECD protein
Fig.11: mouse neurons + KD of mouse NPR

The primary term relates to the formation of the postsynaptic specialization. This is evidenced mainly in Fig.2, 4 and 11. This uses the mouse NPR protein. Annotation is made againse mouse.
